title: The doer should not grade itself
summary: A useful AI evaluation separates the system doing the work from the system judging it.
body_html: If the same model writes an answer and grades the answer, the evaluation can reward style, agreement, or hidden assumptions instead of task success. A better setup defines the claim before the run, separates doer and rater, records what was not measured, and treats uncertainty as part of the result.
key_points: Pre-registering the claim protects the metric from drift.
A not-measured ledger is part of an honest result.
outcome: You can explain why self-grading inflates confidence.
